details: The package contains lib/trap-core.js, a module that imports fs, os, https, and dns and assembles host identifiers (os.hostname(), os.platform(), os.homedir(), process.env.USER, process.cwd()) before issuing https.get / https.request POST calls at multiple sites (lines 267, 274, 292, 293, 346). The file additionally references ping/network-command behavior and walks the filesystem via repeated fs.existsSync probes. The combination of host/user/environment harvesting bound to outbound HTTPS POSTs inside a package whose advertised purpose is LLM context compression has no legitimate explanation — it is the canonical system-information exfiltration shape. Installing or loading this package risks leaking installer host identity and environment data to the destination(s) hardcoded in trap-core.js.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
